$comment: GENERATED by scripts/gen-examples-manifest.ts — do not hand-edit. See packages/core/docs/EXAMPLE-COOKBOOK-CONVENTION.md.
$vendored: VENDORED into web from the schemic repo (main). Driver-verified cookbook. Do not edit by hand; re-sync this whole file when `source.hash` changes upstream (current: a8a72e97e7b23fac). Read via apps/schemic/src/config/examples-catalog.ts.

entries.title: SCHEMAFULL (the default)
entries.code: defineTable("account", { id: s.string(), name: s.string() })
entries.ddl:
DEFINE TABLE account TYPE NORMAL SCHEMAFULL;
DEFINE FIELD name ON TABLE account TYPE string;
entries.note: defineTable is SCHEMAFULL by default — fields are constrained to the declared shape.

entries.title: SCHEMALESS
entries.code: defineTable("blob", { id: s.string() }).schemaless()
entries.ddl: DEFINE TABLE blob TYPE NORMAL SCHEMALESS;
entries.note: Opt out with .schemaless() — records may carry undeclared fields.

entries.title: TYPE ANY
entries.code: defineTable("misc", { id: s.string() }).typeAny()
entries.ddl: DEFINE TABLE misc TYPE ANY SCHEMAFULL;
entries.note: Accept any record shape (.typeAny()).

entries.title: COMMENT
entries.code: defineTable("account", { id: s.string() }).comment("billing accounts")
entries.ddl: DEFINE TABLE account TYPE NORMAL SCHEMAFULL COMMENT "billing accounts";

entries.title: table PERMISSIONS
entries.ddl: DEFINE TABLE doc TYPE NORMAL SCHEMAFULL PERMISSIONS FOR select WHERE true FOR create WHERE $auth.id != NONE FOR update WHERE $auth.id = id.owner FOR delete WHERE $auth.admin = true;
entries.note: Per-operation row guards; an omitted op defaults to NONE.

entries.title: CHANGEFEED
entries.code: defineTable("audited", { id: s.string() }).changefeed("7d", { includeOriginal: true })
entries.ddl: DEFINE TABLE audited TYPE NORMAL SCHEMAFULL CHANGEFEED 7d INCLUDE ORIGINAL;
entries.note: Retain a change feed for the table; INCLUDE ORIGINAL via the option.

entries.title: DROP
entries.code: defineTable("legacy", { id: s.string() }).drop(true)
entries.ddl: DEFINE TABLE legacy TYPE NORMAL DROP SCHEMAFULL;
entries.note: A DROP table discards writes (TYPE NORMAL DROP) — useful to retire a table's data.

entries.title: TYPE RELATION (edge table) with FROM / TO / ENFORCED
entries.code:
defineRelation("likes", { since: s.datetime() })
  .from(defineTable("user", { id: s.string() }))
  .to(defineTable("post", { id: s.string() }))
  .enforced()
entries.ddl:
DEFINE TABLE likes TYPE RELATION FROM user TO post ENFORCED SCHEMAFULL;
DEFINE FIELD since ON TABLE likes TYPE datetime;
entries.note: defineRelation + .from()/.to() restrict endpoints; .enforced() requires both to exist on RELATE.

entries.title: TYPE RELATION — unrestricted endpoints
entries.code: defineRelation("touches", {})
entries.ddl: DEFINE TABLE touches TYPE RELATION SCHEMAFULL;
entries.note: Endpoints are optional; a bare relation links any record to any record.

entries.title: Pre-computed VIEW (AS SELECT)
entries.code: defineView("adults", surql`SELECT name, age FROM user WHERE age >= 18`)
entries.ddl: DEFINE TABLE adults TYPE ANY SCHEMALESS AS SELECT name, age FROM user WHERE age >= 18;
entries.note: defineView emits TYPE ANY SCHEMALESS AS <query>; rows are kept in sync by SurrealDB. No authored fields.

entries.title: Scalars
entries.code:
defineTable("scalars", {
  id: s.string(),
  str: s.string(),
  i: s.int(),
  f: s.float(),
  dec: s.decimal(),
  num: s.number(),
  b: s.boolean(),
  when: s.datetime(),
  uid: s.uuid(),
  raw: s.bytes(),
  dur: s.duration(),
  doc: s.file(),
})
entries.note: Plus aliases: s.int32/uint32/bigint, s.date (datetime).

entries.title: any / null
entries.code: defineTable("loose", { id: s.string(), anything: s.any(), nothing: s.null() })

entries.title: Optionality — option<T> vs T | null vs option<T | null>
entries.note: Kept distinct (absent vs present-null vs both), unlike SQL drivers that collapse them.

entries.title: Containers — array / set (with max), object, tuple
entries.code:
defineTable("containers", {
  id: s.string(),
  tags: s.array(s.string()),
  top3: s.array(s.string(), { max: 3 }),
  uniq: s.set(s.int()),
  coords: s.tuple([s.float(), s.float()]),
  meta: s.object({ k: s.string(), n: s.int() }),
})

entries.title: Literals, enums, scalar unions
Record links — record<table>, multi-table, array<record<…>>
Geometry — bare and the 7 kinds
entries.code:
defineTable("geo", {
  id: s.string(),
  any: s.geometry(),
  pt: s.geometry("point"),
  ln: s.geometry("line"),
  poly: s.geometry("polygon"),
  mpt: s.geometry("multipoint"),
  mln: s.geometry("multiline"),
  mpoly: s.geometry("multipolygon"),
  coll: s.geometry("collection"),
})

entries.title: DEFAULT and DEFAULT ALWAYS
entries.note: Literals stay bare; wrap an expression in surql`…`. ALWAYS re-applies on every update.

entries.title: VALUE and COMPUTED
entries.note: VALUE computes/coerces on write; COMPUTED is a derived (virtual) value.

entries.title: ASSERT — raw and baked $-constraints
entries.code:
defineTable("a", {
  id: s.string(),
  age: s.int().$min(0).$max(120),
  name: s.string().$length(64),
  score: s.float().$gte(0).$lte(1),
  slug: s.string().$assert(surql`$value = /^[a-z-]+$/`),
})
entries.note: $min/$max/$length/$gt/... bake into ASSERT; $assert(surql`…`) is the raw escape.

entries.title: String-format builders (reverse to the builder on pull)
entries.code:
defineTable("fmt", {
  id: s.string(),
  email: s.email(),
  site: s.url(),
  ip: s.ipv4(),
  id2: s.ulid(),
})
entries.note: Each bakes an ASSERT; pull recovers the builder (s.email()) not the raw string ASSERT.

entries.title: READONLY and field COMMENT
FLEXIBLE object
entries.code: defineTable("fx", { id: s.string(), meta: s.object({ k: s.string() }).flexible() })
entries.note: .flexible() (alias .loose()) lets a typed object also carry undeclared keys.

entries.title: Field PERMISSIONS
entries.ddl:
DEFINE TABLE fp TYPE NORMAL SCHEMAFULL;
DEFINE FIELD secret ON TABLE fp TYPE string PERMISSIONS FOR select WHERE $auth.admin = true FOR update WHERE false;
entries.note: Field-level guards: select/create/update (no delete at field level, matching SurrealQL).

entries.title: REFERENCE — record link with ON DELETE
entries.ddl:
DEFINE TABLE ref TYPE NORMAL SCHEMAFULL;
DEFINE FIELD author ON TABLE ref TYPE record<user> REFERENCE ON DELETE CASCADE;
entries.note: Reference integrity: REJECT | CASCADE | UNSET | IGNORE | THEN <expr>.

entries.title: Plain and composite index
entries.code: defineTable("p", { id: s.string(), a: s.string(), b: s.string() }).index("ab", ["a", "b"])

entries.title: UNIQUE (single field via field.unique() or table.index)
entries.code: defineTable("u", { id: s.string(), email: s.string() }).index("uq", ["email"], { unique: true })

entries.title: COUNT (materialized row-count, no FIELDS)
entries.code: defineTable("ct", { id: s.string() }).index("rows", [], { count: true })
entries.ddl:
DEFINE TABLE ct TYPE NORMAL SCHEMAFULL;
DEFINE INDEX rows ON TABLE ct COUNT;

entries.title: COMMENT
entries.code: defineTable("cm", { id: s.string(), email: s.string() }).index("uq", ["email"], { unique: true, comment: "email is unique" })

entries.title: Vector HNSW — minimal (defaults stripped)
entries.code: defineTable("vh", { id: s.string(), emb: s.array(s.float()) }).index("vec", ["emb"], { hnsw: { dimension: 4 } })
entries.note: Only DIMENSION authored; SurrealDB materializes DIST/TYPE/EFC/M/M0/LM — all stripped so it round-trips.

entries.title: Vector HNSW — tuned
entries.code: defineTable("vh2", { id: s.string(), emb: s.array(s.float()) }).index("vec", ["emb"], { hnsw: { dimension: 8, dist: "cosine", type: "f64", efc: 200, m: 16 } })

entries.title: Vector DISKANN — minimal
entries.code: defineTable("vd", { id: s.string(), emb: s.array(s.float()) }).index("vec", ["emb"], { diskann: { dimension: 4 } })

entries.title: FULLTEXT search index + DEFINE ANALYZER
entries.note: A FULLTEXT index deps on its analyzer (emitted first). Default BM25(1.2,0.75) is stripped.

entries.title: Inline event with WHEN + THEN
Event without WHEN (fires on every change)
entries.ddl:
DEFINE TABLE doc TYPE NORMAL SCHEMAFULL;
DEFINE EVENT touch ON TABLE doc THEN UPDATE audit SET at = time::now();

entries.title: Standalone event with an ordered THEN array
entries.ddl: DEFINE EVENT onCreate ON TABLE account WHEN $event = "CREATE" THEN (CREATE log SET account = $after.id), (UPDATE stats SET count += 1);
entries.note: defineEvent declares an event apart from its table; pull regenerates it inline.

entries.title: Function with args, return type, body
entries.code:
defineFunction("greet", { name: s.string() })
  .returns(s.string())
  .body(surql`RETURN "hi " + $name`)
entries.ddl: DEFINE FUNCTION fn::greet($name: string) -> string { RETURN "hi " + $name };

entries.title: Function with PERMISSIONS and COMMENT
entries.code:
defineFunction("tax", { amount: s.float() })
  .returns(s.float())
  .body(surql`RETURN $amount * 0.21`)
  .permissions(surql`$auth.id != NONE`)
  .comment("VAT helper")
entries.ddl: DEFINE FUNCTION fn::tax($amount: float) -> float { RETURN $amount * 0.21 } PERMISSIONS $auth.id != NONE COMMENT "VAT helper";

entries.title: RECORD access (SIGNUP / SIGNIN / DURATION)
entries.code:
defineAccess("user")
  .record()
  .signup(surql`CREATE user SET email = $email, pass = crypto::argon2::generate($pass)`)
  .signin(surql`SELECT * FROM user WHERE email = $email AND crypto::argon2::compare(pass, $pass)`)
  .duration({ token: "1h", session: "12h" })
entries.ddl: DEFINE ACCESS user ON DATABASE TYPE RECORD SIGNUP { CREATE user SET email = $email, pass = crypto::argon2::generate($pass) } SIGNIN { SELECT * FROM user WHERE email = $email AND crypto::argon2::compare(pass, $pass) } DURATION FOR TOKEN 1h, FOR SESSION 12h;

entries.title: RECORD access with AUTHENTICATE
entries.code:
defineAccess("api")
  .record()
  .authenticate(surql`RETURN $auth`)
  .duration({ session: "1d" })
entries.ddl: DEFINE ACCESS api ON DATABASE TYPE RECORD AUTHENTICATE { RETURN $auth } DURATION FOR SESSION 1d;

entries.title: JWT access (validate external tokens)
entries.code: defineAccess("external").jwt({ alg: "HS512", key: "secret" }).onDatabase()
entries.ddl: DEFINE ACCESS external ON DATABASE TYPE JWT ALGORITHM HS512 KEY "secret";
entries.note: Structure (alg + key/url) applies + introspects, but the signing KEY is redacted on pull.

entries.title: BEARER access (API-key grants)
entries.code: defineAccess("apikey").bearer({ for: "user" }).duration({ session: "30d" })
entries.ddl: DEFINE ACCESS apikey ON DATABASE TYPE BEARER FOR USER DURATION FOR SESSION 30d;
entries.note: Subject + duration round-trip; the grant secret is redacted on introspect.

entries.title: Analyzer with tokenizers + filters
entries.ddl: DEFINE ANALYZER english TOKENIZERS BLANK, CLASS FILTERS LOWERCASE, SNOWBALL(ENGLISH);

entries.title: Analyzer with tokenizers only
entries.code: defineAnalyzer("simple", { tokenizers: ["blank"] })
entries.ddl: DEFINE ANALYZER simple TOKENIZERS BLANK;

entries.title: .$surreal(wire, codec) — store an instanceof type as a string
entries.ddl:
DEFINE TABLE wallet TYPE NORMAL SCHEMAFULL;
DEFINE FIELD price ON TABLE wallet TYPE string;
entries.note: App type = Money, wire/DDL type = string; the codec maps both ways. Clears the no-DDL brand.

entries.title: .$surreal on s.custom — store a Set as array<string>
entries.ddl:
DEFINE TABLE bag TYPE NORMAL SCHEMAFULL;
DEFINE FIELD tags ON TABLE bag TYPE array<string>;

entries.title: .$internal() — DB-managed, client-hidden field (PERMISSIONS NONE)
entries.code: defineTable("account", { id: s.string(), passhash: s.string().$internal() })
entries.ddl:
DEFINE TABLE account TYPE NORMAL SCHEMAFULL;
DEFINE FIELD passhash ON TABLE account TYPE string PERMISSIONS NONE;

entries.title: implicit id primary key
entries.code: defineTable("user", { name: s.text() })
entries.note: no PK authored -> the driver adds `"id" text PRIMARY KEY` (mirrors Surreal's record id)

entries.title: composite primary key replaces the implicit id
entries.code: defineTable("member", { org: s.text(), person: s.text() }).primaryKey("org", "person")

entries.title: table-level CHECK
entries.code: defineTable("account", { balance: s.numeric(12, 2) }).check("balance >= 0")
entries.note: emit-faithful; excluded from change-detection (PG rewrites expressions on read)

entries.title: canonical scalars
entries.code:
defineTable("t", {
  name: s.text(),
  count: s.integer(),
  ratio: s.doublePrecision(),
  active: s.boolean(),
  created: s.timestamptz(),
  token: s.uuid(),
})
entries.ddl:
CREATE TABLE "t" (
  "id" text PRIMARY KEY,
  "active" boolean NOT NULL,
  "count" integer NOT NULL,
  "created" timestamp with time zone NOT NULL,
  "name" text NOT NULL,
  "ratio" double precision NOT NULL,
  "token" uuid NOT NULL
);

entries.title: pg-native parameterized types
entries.code:
defineTable("t", {
  label: s.varchar(255),
  price: s.numeric(10, 2),
  big: s.bigint(),
  small: s.smallint(),
  approx: s.real(),
})
entries.ddl:
CREATE TABLE "t" (
  "id" text PRIMARY KEY,
  "approx" real NOT NULL,
  "big" bigint NOT NULL,
  "label" varchar(255) NOT NULL,
  "price" numeric(10, 2) NOT NULL,
  "small" smallint NOT NULL
);
entries.note: varchar(n) / numeric(p,s) preserve their params; bigint/smallint/real are native

entries.title: array column
entries.code: defineTable("t", { tags: s.text().array() })

entries.title: jsonb (object collapses to an opaque jsonb column)
nullability (.optional / .nullable both -> a nullable column)
entries.code: defineTable("t", { age: s.smallint().optional(), bio: s.text().nullable() })

entries.title: identity column (GENERATED ... AS IDENTITY)
entries.code: defineTable("t", { seq: s.integer().$identity("by-default") })

entries.title: default: a JS literal vs sqlExpr() for a SQL expression
entries.note: $default needs sqlExpr() to tell a SQL expr from a literal; emit-faithful, excluded from drift

entries.title: field CHECK (a bare string expression)
entries.code: defineTable("t", { score: s.integer().$check("score >= 0") })

entries.title: generated / computed column (STORED)
entries.note: reference other columns by their (quoted) name; camelCase needs quotes: "unitPrice"

entries.title: column comment (COMMENT ON COLUMN)
entries.code: defineTable("t", { note: s.text().$comment("free text") })
entries.note: emit-only — not introspected back yet

entries.title: column UNIQUE ($unique) -> CREATE UNIQUE INDEX
entries.code: defineTable("user", { email: s.text().$unique() })
entries.note: the $unique convention names it <table>_<col>_key

entries.title: secondary index (.index)
entries.code: defineTable("post", { title: s.text() }).index(["title"])
entries.note: emit-only: non-unique indexes are not introspected back yet, so they do not round-trip (see COVERAGE)

entries.title: composite UNIQUE index
entries.code: defineTable("membership", { org: s.text(), user: s.text() }).index(["org", "user"], { unique: true })

entries.title: foreign key (s.references) -> text column + FK to ref(id)
entries.code:
[
  defineTable("usr", { name: s.text() }),
  defineTable("post", { author: s.references("usr") }),
]
entries.note: the FK is its own kind (deps -> [table, refTable]), so it emits after both tables

entries.title: FK with referential actions (ON DELETE / ON UPDATE)
entries.note: actions canonicalize UPPERCASE; the default NO ACTION is omitted

entries.title: FK via table.record() (reference another table object)
customer / order: FK + CHECK + generated column + unique index
factory: s.$postgres(pgType, codec) (from scratch)
entries.code: defineTable("blob", { raw: s.$postgres("text", z.string()) })

entries.title: chainable: App value -> stored as the wire type (mirrors surreal .$surreal)
entries.note: column emits as the wire type (varchar(32)); the codec maps app<->wire. `Money` is a demo App class
